REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS PETER LOUIE AND VIVIENNE LOUIE'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER LIFTING STAY IS GRANTED. SEPTEMBER 30, 2016 ORDER AND OCTOBER 27, 2016 CONDITIONAL JUDGMENT (SUBJECT TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2016 ORDER) REMAIN IN EFFECT. ON SEPTEMBER 30, 2016 THIS COURT FOUND THAT DEFENDANTS WERE IN BREACH OF CERTAIN OBLIGATIONS UNDER THEIR SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE COURT GAVE DEFENDANTS AN OPPORTUNITY TO RECTIFY THE BREACHES SUBJECT TO REQUIREMENTS LISTED IN DETAIL IN THE ORDER. THE LAST PAYMENT UNDER THE ORDER WAS DUE ON OCTOBER 30, 2016. ON OCTOBER 27, 2016 THE COURT EXECUTED A JUDGMENT SPECIFICALLY SUBJECTING IT TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2016 ORDER. THE JUDGMENT WAS NOT FINAL AND WAS TO BECOME EFFECTIVE ONLY UPON LIFTING OF THE STAY FOR DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH SEPTEMBER 30, 2016 ORDER. ON JANUARY 6, 2017 PLAINTIFF'S APPEARED AT AN UNOPPOSED EX-PARTE SEEKING TO LIFT A STAY DUE TO THE EXPIRATION OF TIME TO FILE AN APPEAL, NOT DUE TO DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH SEPTEMBER 30, 2016 ORDER. SINCE THERE IS NO FINAL JUDGMENT IN THIS ACTION, CCP 1008 MAY PROPERLY BE CONSIDERED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN NEW CIRCUMSTANCES. JANUARY 6, 2017 ORDER IS VACATED. THE COURT RECONSIDERS JANUARY 6, 2017 APPLICATION AND DENIES IT. NOTHING IN THE APPLICATION INDICATES THAT DEFENDANTS ARE IN VIOLATION OF SEPTEMBER 30, 2016, AND THEREFORE OCTOBER 27, 2016 JUDGEMENT IS NOT EFFECTIVE. THE STAY ON ENTRY OF JUDGMENT REMAINS EFFECT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) NO LATER THAN 3/7/17. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO DEFENDANTS PETER LOUIE AND VIVIENNE LOUIE'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RESTRICTING DISCOVERY, DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS WITHDRAWN HIS FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO PETER LOUIE. IF DEFENDANT VIVIENNE LOUIE SITS FOR HER DEPOSITION ON DECEMBER 3 AND 4, PLAINTIFF SHALL WITHDRAW HIS FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO VIVIENNE LOUIE WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO OR WAIVER OF PLAINTIFF'S RIGHTS UNDER CCP 2030.010 ET SEQ. TO PROPOUND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AT A LATER DATE. OTHERWISE, DEFENDANTS SHALL RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S OTHER WRITTEN DISCOVERY PROPOUNDED AUGUST 21, 2015 NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 28, 2015. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS DID NOT PROPERLY ENGAGE IN A MEET-AND-CONFER PROCESS TO ATTEMPT TO INFORMALLY RESOLVE THIS DISPUTE AND IT WAS DEFENDANTS' OWN FAILURE TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION THAT CAUSED PLAINTIFF TO PROPOUND THE WRITTEN DISCOVERY. DEFENDANTS AND DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL ARE SANCTIONED FOR MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY SANCTIONED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,200, PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 21, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: CONOR MOORE, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Lifting Stay
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
PETER LOUIE (415-874-5528) Defendant/Moving party PETER LOUIE.
No other appearances.

Defendant did not comply with Tentative Ruling Rule. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS PETER LOUIE and VIVIENNE LOUIE's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Lifting Stay is GRANTED. September 30, 2016 order and October 27, 2016 conditional judgment (subject to September 30, 2016 order) remain in effect. On September 30, 2016 this Court found that Defendants were in breach of certain obligations under their settlement agreement. The Court gave defendants an opportunity to rectify the breaches subject to requirements listed in detail in the order. The last payment under the order was due on October 30, 2016. On October 27, 2016 the Court executed a judgment specifically subjecting it to September 30, 2016 order. The judgment was not final and was to become effective only upon lifting of the stay for Defendant's failure to comply with September 30, 2016 order. On January 6, 2017 Plaintiff's appeared at an unopposed ex-parte seeking to lift a stay due to the expiration of time to file an appeal, not due to Defendant's failure to comply with September 30, 2016 order. Since there is no final judgment in this action, CCP 1008 may properly be considered. Defendants have shown new circumstances. January 6, 2017 order is vacated. The Court reconsiders January 6, 2017 application and denies it. Nothing in the application indicates that Defendants are in violation of September 30, 2016, and therefore October 27, 2016 judgement is not effective. The stay on entry of judgment remains effect. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) no later than 3/7/17. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
